ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Agi azeegyo eboa la su edi i, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu isu ya abasɛ Yosef ní kulila mɛ. Lɛ akpɛ ɔna ní yɛ lipo si, “Yosef, Dawid yebie, wukíse lifu ní Maria kugbanɔɔ su u, lɛ ese linu si yɛ limɛ nɛ ɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ lidɔ ɔ.
translated: But while he was thinking about these things, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the child conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Niteme liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ Yeremya apani aplɛ ɛ,
translated: The word of the Lord came to Jeremiah again, saying,

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Ní Herodes kutseo kede e, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu isu ya abasi Yosef ní kulila mɛ ní Egipte,
translated: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Pɔ̀ gi Yosef enu si Arxelao yɛ ɛɛŋa kukusi yo ní yɛka Herodes kutse o kede ní Yudea ɛ, ese lifu ne ki niklɔ kutrɛtrɛ. Agi bɛkpɛ yɛ ɔsã ní kulilaa mɛ loso e, ekyi atrɛ ní Galilea kesea mɛ.
translated: But when Joseph heard that Archelaus was reigning in Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he withdrew to the district of Galilee.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Mlabitɛ eboa la gi aabasɛ si mlekyi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: The children went to the farm yesterday but they did not see anything.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Lɛ ole ɔtɔ ɛpani dɔni lifunu ne si, “Mobi ye liɛ ya yɛ madimɛ nɔ yɛ liboe liku me aŋu u!”
translated: "And then the woman said to her husband, 'Please go and get some palm wine for me to drink!'"

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Asɛ ní Nazaret lɛ adzɛ eze ní Kapernaum, gi onu ɔma ɔtɔ ní Galilea limu ne kutu ní Zebulon ni Naftali kuseo me e.
translated: And leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which is by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mlalɛ ŋwisi kani aki kelea mɛ. Ɔma gi bɛtsa ní lito aʋa ɛ, óʋikpo o.
translated: The child is not here today. If you see him on the way, greet him.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “Xe ɔnutɔ akɔ liboe ní wɔ su ní otsixwakɔ ɔ, xe lipoe le lilɛ ɛ, ye liboe le nì yɛ xe litsixwawe le liana a. Xegi aní nite e, xe mleku otsixwaklɔ ɛ, aakɔ̀ wɔ kukpɛ awla ki otsixwa yɛ, kɔ yɛ kyɛ akɔ wɔ akpɛ awla aki bewukpɛ ʋa ɛ. Niteme si biakpɛ wɔ ligba.
translated: If a person goes to the farm and returns and is tired, he eats and rests, and when he is strong he goes back to the farm. If it is not so, he will be tired, and will not be able to do the work well, or will not be able to finish the work he started. That is how it is with life.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Sugba sugba ɛ, níklɔ wɔaze kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ gi wɔatsa liʋɛ kakaa gi bɛnya ɛki wɔ ɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní ádzɛboe nì enyimeboe su u
translated: The elders say, if you want to build a strong house, you must first lay a good foundation. Jesus is the foundation and the cornerstone of our faith.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Mleenu gi bɛpani si, ‘Mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba atɔ mlekyri mlɔ ahwafianɛ ma.’
translated: "The child said, 'I will go to the farm and bring back some firewood.'"

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Mlaki si mlɔ kizezee me kiflɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne aflɔ kinɛ ɛ.
translated: The woman went to the market and bought fish, then she went home and cooked it for her children.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Mledi lɛ aʋa si, mlakíbitɛ mlɔ eboe gbã na ní banima aŋu mɛ tɔ bamɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ xe mlɛbitɛ nite, Ɔkaɛ ní lifunu ne ɔ́tratsa liʋɛ tɔtɔ ɛki mlɔ ɔ.
translated: When the day comes, everyone will gather together and talk about what happened. If they agree, the elders and the youth will eat and drink together.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Xe wɔɛxwa Aya ɛ, wukízeege eboe la epio ŋwisi kiliɛ gi babubitɛ ba bizɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si bebu si xegi ba eboe la ɛsrɛ ɛ, kɔ ba Aya enu aki ba ɛ.
translated: When Aya was young, her grandmother would tell her stories about the old days, and she loved to listen to them and remember them, so Aya grew up with them.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Lɛ “Xe mlɛkɔ banima eboe zimizi na ní mlɔ su i, kyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka ní lifunu ne fɛ aakɔ̀ mlɔ ani na kyɛ mlɔ.
translated: “If you do not forgive people their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins.”

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Pɔ xe mlákɔ banima eboe zimizi na akyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka fɛ ɔ́trakɔ mlɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ mlɔ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní onugu nyanya su
translated: If you do good things for others, God will also do good things for you. Jesus loves everyone and wants us to love each other.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Mledi liwɔ lɛ mɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ; kuʋíkpɛ bidɔ loo vini bidɔ kɔbla ní owle me, pɔ fɛ ɛ mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e izeedi kɔ aʋa. Mláŋa kedzi asani kɔ na a?
translated: The child is not here today; the children who used to play here have gone to the farm, so I saw Oka with his friend as they were coming. Should I wait here or go?

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Lɛ ese linu si, ɔnɔ kakaa gi eeʋi bidɔ ɛ, bizɛki yɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛɛpɛ bidɔ ɛ, aamɔ ʋɛ ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa gi aasa opupo lo e, biadra lɔ ɔki yɛ.
translated: If you do this, you will get some things, if you do that, you will get other things, but if you do nothing, you will get nothing at all.

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Nite si maasi ba wee si, “Méte mlo liwewe e, Mlasɛ ní mɛ su, mlɔ banɔ dovo ba!” Bagbawa tiaʋa ba a
translated: "Then the woman said, 'I have heard your words, but if I go, I will not return!' The elders were surprised.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Abitɛ lɛ nite tɔ yiki liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya apani i liba lɛ mɛ si, “Akɔ blɔ kubotsobotsoo atrɔ isu ya aʋa lɛ etu blɔ bànya ʋa.” Banɔ tiaʋa bɛpɛ si biakuni Yesu kude
translated: This happened to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah, who said, “A voice is calling out in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way of the Lord.’” Some people came to ask Jesus

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yesu emu eku ní katapokpaa mɛ lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bekuni yɛ kude e.
translated: Jesus himself will come again to take all those who believe in him.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Iha kisrɛsrɛ ɛtɔ iizɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ní niklɔ ɔza lɔ.
translated: The children ran and played in the yard this morning.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Agi Yesu eti eku ní kepea mɛ ɛ, benyime tiaʋa ba klɔ gi ba bimbi ʋe bini i bɛba yɛ klɔ, lɛ eʋi ba si, “Mlɛzi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa si maatani mlɔ bimbi ʋe kudra na?” Bebu le onu si, “Ee, Ɔkaɛ.”
translated: When Jesus was sitting there, many children came to him and the people said to them, "Why are you bringing these children here?" But he said, "Let them come." They answered, "Yes, Teacher."

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Lɛ asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ bidɔ mɛ bisrɛ, pɔ̀ baxwɛwa gi biakpɛ ʋɛ likpɔ básrɛ ɛ.
translated: If you do something good for someone, the person will remember you and thank you, but if you do something bad, they will never forget it.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Beneminyime biakpɛ ba beneminyime ní awla tɔ beye; lɛ bebika batɔ kyɛ biabitɛ nite ke tsini ba bebi ba su u, bebi biayɔ kedu ní ba bapɔnɛ ba su gi biaki biaye ba a.
translated: The ancestors say that what the ancestors have said is true; so they say that if someone does not listen to what they say, that person will not know what to do in life.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: Ɔdɔkpasibi ɔtɔtɔ óʋikeme ɔsani yɛ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ; lɛ ɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ɔtɔtɔ óʋimu ɔsani yɛ ɔkaɛ ɛ.
translated: Love is patient, love is kind; it does not envy, it does not boast.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Tiasɛ kura abia taʋa izɛɛzɛ ɛ? Pɔ̀ libia ɛtɔtɔ litrawɔli dɔ kesea xegi mlɔ ɔka Aya ɔ́ki kudeonu u.
translated: Did you see the woman who came yesterday? She was wearing a beautiful dress and Aya was sitting next to her.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Lɛ loso e, mlakíse lifu u. Lɛ ese linu si, mlɛŋa kedzi isani abia likpi. Yesu yɛ su kudradra ni yɛ kugbegbe
translated: So it is, the wind blows. As you hear its sound, you do not know where it comes from or where it is going. So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Mlakígyo le edi si kusuyɔ mamanɔ ní kelea mɛ ɛ. Owo, mámani kusuyɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ kusi lɛlɛ kwitraze e.
translated: The child is here and wants to eat food. But, there is no food, so he/she is just sitting quietly.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔniliɛ gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aalila yɛ ligbã nɛ kɔkpɛ mɛ loso e, aabu le. Liʋɛtsatsa ní Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi kuzɛɛzɛ loso
translated: The woman who was sick for a long time was healed. When the woman was healed for a long time and went to the town, people were happy. This is how Jesus heals those who have faith.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Agi Yohanes aba ɛ azaanya onugu lo lɛ ɔ́ŋwɛ wain kyɛ ɛ; lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa apani si, ‘Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔkpasi ní yɛ mɛ!’
translated: Then John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, ‘Look, the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!’

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: “ ‘kwikpɛ kudzɛgbanɔ idzi iki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mleyo o. Kwikpɛ ibemidzi i ki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mlébemi i.’
translated: "A person who does not listen to advice gets into trouble, but one who listens is saved."

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Abitɛ lɛ nite atɔ yikɔ ebu í basɛ si, liboe lɛ gi Aya apani agani yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya aʋa ɛ liba lɛ mɛ si:
translated: The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light, those who lived in a land of deep darkness—on them light has shined.

ref: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
text: Lɛ ese linu si wɔ tutɔ wɔ eboe la biakɔ tsã litsi le ɔki wɔ, lɛ wɔ tutɔ wɔ eboe la biakɔ bu wɔ lipɔ.” Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ ni Farisianɛatɔ beʋi i dɔni Yesu awla si ɔbitɛ linugutseboe
translated: He also told this parable to some who trusted in themselves that they were righteous and despised others: Two men went up into the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Kiliɛ gi Yona eze ní bosoe yɛ lipo le me ewe tata ni elesi tata la ɛ, nite ke Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye fɛ aaze ní sise se me, ewe tata ni elesi tata a.
translated: When Jonah was inside the belly of the fish for three days and three nights, just as the Son of Man will be in the heart of the earth for three days and three nights.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
text: Atɔ ɛdɔ ni bibu ʋe me lɛ bibu ʋe biyɛ xe biwe amu vuvu na aʋa lɛ etse e.
translated: There are two types of people who do not like to eat yam that is not well cooked.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Matrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa aki mlɔ si, Aya yɛ banɔ ni beboedoplɛ glegle bɛpɛ si bɛmɔ kidiliɛ gi mlɛɛmɔ ɛ, pɔ̀ bámɔ kudeonu nite e; lɛ bɛpɛ dzɛ si benu eboa la gi mleenu u, pɔ̀ bámɔ kudeonu nite e.” Yesu ebu abee ese ní amu na ogluglu ye su u
translated: “Teacher, this woman was caught in the act of adultery. In the Law, Moses commanded us to stone such women. Now what do you say?” They were using this question as a trap, in order to have a basis for accusing him. But Jesus bent down and started to write on the ground with his finger.

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: Pɔ̀ gi amɔ liwɔ gagla lɛ ɛ, ese lifu ne ko lɛ ekpese kuwɔli uku ní kuni yo me. Lɛ Petro akpɛ ozi lo si, “Zɛ kímɛ Ɔkaɛ!”
translated: But when he saw the wind, he was afraid and, beginning to sink, cried out, “Lord, save me!”

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Yesu asi ba si, “Kɔlɔ ɛ, mlakɔ la amani mɛ ní ya.”
translated: Jesus said, “Child, get up and come to me.”

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: Agi aya banima pɔ ɛ, emu atrɛ ní ketokpa katɔ aʋa yɛ tètè lɛ azɛɛxwa Aya ɛ. Gi ɔlagɔ lɔ ɛna ɛ, Yesu tètè ko eze niklɔ;
translated: Long ago, there was a certain woman, her story is told from the beginning up to now. In those days, Jesus was also born;

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Lɛ bedu litukpo kesea ɛki yɛ si ɔki kude tɔ banama gi bɛnya ɛ, bagbɔni yɛ liwu le ɔgyurɔ lɔ ko; lɛ banama petee gi bɛgbɔni liwu le e, bɛyɔ ɔ.
translated: If someone throws a big stone and it does not hit anyone, it just lies there; but if it hits someone, it causes pain.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Pɔ̀ mlɛbasɛ bidɔ si, xe kidɔ kiliɛ obi ɔtɔ awla gi aatani kɛ kukɔpɔni yɛka loo yene e pɔ̀ xe apani si, ‘Aya bidɔ binu i,’
translated: If you see a child there, and the child takes something that does not belong to them and hides it under their clothes, you will say to the child, ‘This is not your thing.’

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: “Mlakífuɛ ni ba; Kuŋupo benu xe bɛgbani kuŋupo o, lɛ xe kaŋupo kɛgbani kaŋupo e, ba tiaʋa pɔ́ biìdɔ eku ní lizokpo me.”
translated: The chief said; If someone greets you, and you do not respond to the greeting, then you have not respected the greeting, and you have not shown respect to the person, so do not expect good things in the community.

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba yɛ klɔ lɛ beʋi ye si, “Wote si eboa la gi wɔpani i eeyeʋi Farisianɛ ma na a?”
translated: Then the tax collectors came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher not pay the temple tax?”

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: Kiboebitɛ liɛ ya elu i, kɛ kizelulu ɔnɔ. Pɔ̀ tɔ ŋa bidɔ awla moplo e, biʋílulu ɔnɔ.” Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ yɛ kuziŋa
translated: The children went to the house, and they greeted their mother. Then they sat down and ate food, and they thanked their mother. After that, they went outside to play.

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ɔdzɛ wɔ kuziŋaa kwikeme koŋ! Kidiliɛ gi wɔɛpɛ ɛ kiaba kɛ mɛ ɔki wɔ.” Lɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɖi i yebidzɛ ayɔ. Yesu ayɔ nì banyanya glegle
translated: Then Jesus said to him, “Be quiet! Come out of him!” The evil spirit shook the man violently and came out of him with a loud shriek. The people were all so amazed.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Banima inugu itse gi bɛmɔ imumu ne gi iigu kunugu yo, banɔ klɔʋiklɔʋi ba gi bɛɛga, bafa ba bɛyɔ ɛga, lɛ kuŋupoo fɛ kwimɔ ɛ. Nite si bɛkpɔ̀ Israel Aya. Yesu aga ni banɔ akpe tiene
translated: The people were amazed at his teaching and his words, the children listened to him, the women welcomed him, and everyone was happy. This is what happened in Israel. Jesus did many good things for the people.

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Eliya yɛ aatu kuba, kɔ akpɛ kidɔ kakaa likpɔ aplɛ ɛ.
translated: Jesus answered them, “Elijah is indeed coming, and he will restore everything.”

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: lɛ apani si, “Ɔkaɛ, di mobinyimee asigaga! Kukpakpanya kuli ní yɛ su u, gi izeegleʋe eku kifu nì kuni me e, nite si izɛɛmɔ ɔzia glegle.
translated: And the woman said, “Alright, do not worry! Wisdom is like a river, it flows where it wants, and whoever drinks from it becomes bright.”

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: lɛ ese linu si lifunu ne kaʋaŋaa kalɛ nite e: ɔʋaŋa ɔtɔ eze e; lɛ liwe etɔ lɛ ɛ, atrɔ si yɛ aga kibɔɛ eboe la mɛ ni yɛ banɔsumu ba.
translated: If someone wants to do something good, let him do it; but if he does not, let him be, because everyone has their own way of thinking.

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: “Pɔ̀ ɔ́zɛ lɛ ɔki yɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, aki bɛkpɛ yɛ ligba kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi aatsa liʋɛ nɛ ɔki yɛ pɔ́ ɛ.
translated: He/she is not here. That is why, when you came, you did not see the person you were looking for.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Mlenu lɛ ese si, eboe glegle alɛ gi la loso onyime ɔ́tratani ɔdzɛ kugbanɔ ɔ: benyimeatɔ ɛ, bɛpɔ ba si bawɔ ɔdzɛ mɔgbanɔ ɔ; batɔ ɛ banɔ bɛbitɛ ba nite; lɛ benyimeatɔ fɛ bágbani badzɛ kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa loso o. Ɔniliɛ gi aatani lɛ ese kunu i tɔ ɔzɛ lɛ.” Yesu akrira banuvɔ ba
translated: The kingdom of heaven is like this: a man sowed good seed in his field; but while people were sleeping, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and went away. When the wheat sprouted and formed heads, then the weeds also appeared. The owner's servants came to him and said, "Sir, didn't you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?" Jesus told them this parable.

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: Kɔ bɛkɔ yɛ kpɛ́ awla ɛki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ. Ba kyɛ ɛ, biakpɛ yɛ kakpaa kɔ bekusi ye e; gɔgɔ ɛ, kɔ beku ye ní ose lo su i. Pɔ̀ liwe tanɛ we le aʋa ɛ, Aya aayɔ ni yɛ ɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ.” Yakobo ni Yohanes ba onee eʋi kupɔnɔ aki yɛ bebi tiaʋa ba
translated: They will go up to Jerusalem. There, the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the law. translated: Jesus went to Jerusalem, to the temple; when he arrived there, the priests and the elders of the people came to him as he was teaching, and said, “By what authority are you doing these things? And who gave you this authority?”

translated: God gave us hands so that we can work and feet so that we can walk, but He did not give us wings to fly in the sky.

translated: The child took a stone and threw it, and it made a loud noise. When he threw it again at the ground, the noise was even louder.

translated: If the elders are together, they say this proverb: the child resembles those who gave birth to him, the child does not resemble those who raised him.

translated: The woman took the fruit and ate it, and she also gave some to her husband who was with her, and he ate it. Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they realized they were naked.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.

translated: And the name that was given to him, ‘The Saviour’, is the same name by which Christ is called.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba! Mlɛdzi kaale ni kaʋa tɔ mleekyi ɔnɔ, pɔ̀ xe mlemɔ ɔnɔ ɛki Aya ɛ, kɔ mlɛbitɛ yɛ ɔnɔ zimizi gi aasɔ̀ ni kifumoni ye me sani mlɔ tutɔ lɔ ɛ!

translated: Bring the old mat and the old mat bag! Or should I take the one that is here and the other one that is up there?

text: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ abee si lɛ loso e, mleze ni aŋu lɛ ese linu si mléte liwe le e loo ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɔ, gi maadziba ɛ. Yesu ebu abee ni banɔsumu tiata ba su

translated: Then Peter answered him, “Even if everyone else abandons you, I will never leave you!” The disciples all said the same thing. Jesus prays at Gethsemane.

translated: The children are playing under the big tree. When the big tree sheds its leaves, we gather them and use them to make compost, so the tree will continue to grow well.

translated: And then, they took the big basket and put the ripe plantains inside so that everyone could see them clearly.

translated: The children ran quickly to the riverbank. They were happy to see the water flowing, and they played there until the sun set.

translated: When Herod heard this, he said, "John, whom I beheaded, has been raised from the dead." For Herod himself had given orders to have John arrested, and he had him bound and put in prison. ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.

translated: The Pharisees and the Jews gathered, and the elders and the teachers of the law came together, and they brought him before their council.

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Lɛ Yesu ebu lɛ onu si, “Yesaya liboe le ligyɔ koŋ gi amɔ liboe edo aplɛ ní mlɔ su i! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ mlenu, kiliɛ gi aŋwlimi aplɛ si i: Aya si, “ ‘Banɔ ba ya ɛ, enugumeboe ko bɛkɔ ɛlɔ mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ ba ina nɛ kɔ ívu me kura.
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you hypocrites, as it is written: ‘These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me. They worship me in vain; their teachings are merely human rules.’”

text: kɔ libasɛ si mlɛki yɛ kudeo si ɔkipɔni yɛka loo yene e.
